title: Search shows wrong results — finding archived tasks instead of active ones
body: Something is wrong with search. When I search for tasks, I'm getting results that include old archived tasks that should be hidden, and I'm not seeing some active tasks that I know exist. For example, I searched for 'Q2 planning' and got an archived task from last year but not the active one I created yesterday. This is happening for all my searches. My teammate says she sees the same thing.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this! To make sure I understand the expected behavior: when you search for tasks, should archived tasks **never** appear in results, or should they appear but be ranked below active tasks?

A) Archived tasks should be completely excluded from search results by default
B) Archived tasks should appear, but only after all active matches
C) There's a filter or setting I've toggled to hide archived tasks, but it's being ignored
D) Something else

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: It's option A — by default, search should only show active tasks. There's a filter that lets you toggle whether to include archived tasks, but the default is 'active tasks only.' The weird thing is, it seems like the filter is working backwards. When I set it to show only active tasks, I get archived ones, and when I switch it to show archived tasks, I see my active ones instead. It started about three days ago, right after the system was updated to v2.3.1.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Search filter for archived/active tasks is inverted since v2.3.1","problem":"The search filter that controls whether archived or active tasks are shown is working backwards. When set to 'active tasks only' (the default), search returns archived tasks. When set to include archived tasks, it returns active ones instead. This affects all searches for at least two confirmed users and began immediately after the v2.3.1 update.","root_cause_hypothesis":"A logic inversion was introduced in v2.3.1 — most likely a flipped boolean, negated condition, or swapped enum mapping in the search filter code that determines which task status to include/exclude.","reproduction_steps":["Update to v2.3.1","Create or identify a known active task (e.g., titled 'Q2 planning')","Search for that task with the default filter (active tasks only)","Observe that archived tasks appear but the known active task does not","Toggle the filter to include archived tasks","Observe that the active task now appears instead"],"environment":"TaskFlow v2.3.1 (regression not present before this version)","severity":"high","impact":"All users searching for tasks see incorrect results by default — active tasks are hidden and archived tasks are surfaced. This directly undermines core search functionality and likely affects every user on v2.3.1.","recommended_fix":"Diff the search/filter logic between v2.3.0 and v2.3.1. Look for an inverted boolean condition, flipped enum value, or negated predicate in the code path that applies the archived/active filter to search queries. Fix the inversion and verify with the reproduction steps above.","proposed_test_case":"Given tasks in both active and archived states with overlapping names: (1) verify default search returns only active matches, (2) verify toggling the filter to include archived tasks returns both, (3) verify toggling to archived-only returns only archived matches. Assert filter values map to the correct query predicates.","information_gaps":["Whether the issue affects all users globally or only a subset (two confirmed so far)","Whether the inversion also affects other filter dimensions (e.g., by project, by assignee) or only the archived/active filter"]}
